root_text.sn34.55:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.
root_text.sn34.55:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
root_text.sn34.55:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.
root_text.sn34.55:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca.
root_text.sn34.55:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
root_text.sn34.55:1.9: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn34.55:1.10: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro cā”ti.
root_text.sn34.55:1.12: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.sn34.55:1.4: One meditator is skilled in practicing persistently for immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.5: One meditator is skilled in doing what’s conducive to immersion but not in practicing persistently for it.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.6: One meditator is skilled neither in practicing persistently for immersion nor in doing what’s conducive to it.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.7: One meditator is skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.8: Of these, the meditator skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.9: From a cow comes milk, from milk comes curds, from curds come butter, from butter comes ghee, and from ghee comes cream of ghee. And the cream of ghee is said to be the best of these.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.10: In the same way, the meditator skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.”
translation_text.sn34.55:1.11: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.12: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn34.55:1.14: (All should be told in full as per the fifty-fifth discourse.)
translation_text.sn34.55:2.5: The Linked Discourses on Absorption are complete.
translation_text.sn34.55:3.7: The Book of the Aggregates is finished.
